name: ROGAN BOSH!
description: This is our take on a Kashmiri speciality curry. It's meant to be rich and filling, and we love using our favourite vegetables to make it. But you could use different vegetables if you prefer something else on the menu or what you have in the cupboard. Serve with Naan (see page 207) or on its own for a lighter dish.
method: Liquidiser | Large frying pan on medium-high heat | Large saucepan with a lid

Peel the garlic and ginger and put them into the liquidiser. Top up the items from 2-3 of the chillies (remove the seeds for a milder flavour). Blend until smooth to make a paste (you may need to add a small splash of water and blend to a smooth paste). Add the tomato puree and water and blend to a smooth paste mixture.

Trim the aubergine and cut it into 3cm cubes. Add 2 tablespoons of oil to the frying pan and put the aubergine in. Add the cinnamon stick and cardamom pods and fry until golden and a little charred at the edges.
While the aubergine is cooking, put the cardamom pods in a mortar and pestle and bash them to release the seeds (or use the end of a rolling pin). Discard the shells. Peel and finely chop the onion.

When the aubergine has cooked, turn the heat to medium and add the remaining oil to the pan along with the cardamom, peppercorns, bay leaf and cinnamon stick. Add the chopped onion and a good pinch of sugar. Reduce the heat to medium and sauté for about 10-15 minutes, stirring the onions until they are soft and golden. Add a splash of water if the onions start to catch.

Add the ginger-garlic-chilli liquidiser to the saucepan. Add the ground cumin and coriander and mix everything together well. Put the pan on medium-high heat and fry for 5 minutes, stirring regularly. Add the paprika and sugar and cook the tomato and coconut yoghurt and stir it in (if it's too thick, add a little water to be consistency you want a thick consistency). Cover with the lid and cook on medium heat for 5 minutes.

Rip the stem from the remaining chilli, cut it in half lengthways and remove the seeds to make it a milder flavour, then slice finely. Taste the curry and season with salt. Garam masala as necessary. Serve up on to bowls or plates, sprinkled with a little fresh coriander, desiccated coconut flakes and the finely sliced chilli.
